intro: Acampe hayatae, also known as the Turkish orchid, is a species of orchid native to the mountainous regions of Turkey. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Acampe hayatae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Acampe hayatae is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or syrup, and is commonly used to treat respiratory infections, bronchitis, and other respiratory disorders.
